name: Dowry Harassment Checklist
description: Action steps for victims of dowry harassment and related crimes

base_steps.action: Document all harassment incidents and threats
details: Create detailed written record of each incident with date, time, location, and witnesses
Record all threatening phone calls, messages, and communications
Take screenshots of WhatsApp, emails, and other digital communications
Photograph any physical injuries or property damage
Store evidence in secure location (cloud backup + physical copies)
Identify safe place to stay if needed
Inform trusted family members or friends of situation
Keep mobile phone charged and emergency contacts ready
Consider staying at relative's house or safe shelter

base_steps.action: Consult with qualified lawyer specializing in dowry/women's issues
details: Contact women's legal aid organization or NGO
Schedule immediate consultation (many offer free/subsidized help)
Bring all evidence documents and communications
Discuss immediate protective measures (restraining order, safe place order)
Understand available legal options under IPC 498A and Dowry Prohibition Act

base_steps.action: File First Information Report (FIR) at police station
details: Go to nearest police station with lawyer (if possible)
Narrate complete incident with all details
Insist on FIR registration under IPC 498A and other applicable sections
Request copy of FIR for your records
Get the police station's complaint/FIR number
Note the investigating officer's name and contact

base_steps.action: Apply for Domestic Violence Protection Order
details: File application under Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act
Request interim protection order immediately
Ask for shelter home order if needed
Request compensation order
Attend all hearings with lawyer
base_steps.reference_law: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act 2005

details: Prepare list of witnesses (family members, neighbors, friends who saw incidents)
Collect written statements from witnesses
Get medical certificates if there are injuries
Collect financial documents (marriage expenses, gifts, dowry records)
Get communications evidence (printouts, screenshots, recordings if legal)

India.action: Report to Police Women's Helpline (if available in your state)
details: Call women's helpline number for your state (varies by state)
Report incident and get guidance
Ask for women police officer assistance
Get helpline reference number

India.action: Contact NGO/Women's Organization for additional support
details: Reach out to local women's rights organizations
Get counseling and emotional support
Explore legal aid (often free for dowry cases)
Join support groups with other survivors
